sections.title: Holiday Vocabulary
points: Words like VACATION, PEN-PAL, and RURAL help describe the holiday story.
A PEN-PAL is a friend you write to, often from far away.
RURAL describes a village or countryside place.

sections.title: Nouns and Verbs
points: A NOUN names a person, place, or thing, such as FARM or CHORES.
A VERB shows an action, such as SCRUB, DIG, or PLAN.
Sentences often use both a noun and a verb together.

sections.text: Tip: If a word tells you 'what is happening', it is usually a verb. If it names someone or something, it is usually a noun.

cards.fact: A break from school or work, often for travel or rest.
cards.mnemonic: Vacation = time away from regular routine.

cards.fact: A friend you write letters to, usually in another place.
cards.mnemonic: Pen-pal uses a pen to make a new pal.

cards.fact: A place in the countryside, away from the city.
